turns.content: Hello! I'm an AI assistant here to help you narrow down the Material requirements for Grade selection. Could you please tell me which application you're considering?

To better understand your requirements for the UTH connector: 1. What is the exact use of the polymer in your UTH connector? For example, is it for the connector housing, insulation, sealing, or another component? 2. Where will this connector be used in your solution? Is it for automotive wiring harnesses, sensor connections, power distribution, or another specific end-use? Once you provide this information, I'll ask about the minimum wall thickness, preferred base materials, and whether you're open to alternative material suggestions.

Thank you for clarifying that your application is a sensor connector housing for under-the-hood automotive use. To proceed, could you please provide the following details: 1. What is the minimum wall thickness of your sensor connector housing? 2. Do you have any preferred base materials for this application (such as specific polymers or brands)? 3. Are you open to alternative material suggestions if your preferred material is not available or optimal? These details will help ensure the material suggestions are tailored to your requirements.
i dont need ul properties and open to suggestions

Here are the key material requirements (CTQs) assumed for your sensor connector housing: 1. Injection molding required 2. Hydrolysis resistance 3. Heat stabilized or stable to heat 4. Thermal shock resistance 5. Low warpage 6. Electrical insulation 7. Chemical resistance to automotive fluids (oil, fuel vapors) 8. High flow for processing Are all of these requirements correct for your application? Please confirm or let me know if any should be changed.

Here are potential grade options for your sensor connector housing under-the-hood: 1. Zytel® HTN51G35HSL NC010 – PPA 35% GF, hydrolysis & heat stabilized, lubricated; excellent chemical, thermal, and dimensional stability; injection molding; natural color preferred. Widely used in automotive connector housings. 2. Zytel® HTN51G45HSL NC010 – PPA 45% GF for added rigidity and stability; same benefits as above; natural color preferred. 3. CELANYL® A3 HHR3 GF30 BK 9005 – PA66 30% GF, superior long-term heat aging & hydrolysis resistance; designed for oils/coolant exposure; robust connector choice. 4. CELANYL® A3 HHR3 GF35 BK 9005 – PA66 35% GF variant for higher stiffness; same HHR3 benefits. 5. Zytel® 70G25HSLR NC010 – PA66 25% GF, heat/hydrolysis stabilized; proven in automotive connectors; good flow; natural color preferred. 6. FORTRON® 1130L4 – PPS 30% GF, excellent heat/chemical resistance; very low moisture uptake; high flow; for severe environments and tight stability needs. 7. FORTRON® HY 1130L4 – PPS 30% GF, improved flow/hydrolysis; similar use case to 1130L4. 8. FORTRON® 6850L6 – PPS high mineral/glass, lowest warpage; for thin walls/precision under severe conditions. These grades may be suitable for your application, but final material selection and product design decisions should be based on your specific requirements and testing. Please ensure compliance with all applicable standards and regulations. Celanese does not manufacture or certify products for these applications. Note: This recommendation is generated by an AI assistant and should be considered purely informational.
